sent1: O garoto tava brincando na rua até de noite.
sent1_en: The kid was playing in the street until nighttime.
sent2: Esse garoto é muito esperto pra idade dele.
sent2_en: This kid is very smart for his age.
notes: Also 'garota' for girl; extremely common everyday word.

sent1: Cadê meu celular, alguém viu?
sent1_en: Where's my phone, has anyone seen it?
sent2: Cadê a Marina? Ela ia chegar cedo.
sent2_en: Where's Marina? She was supposed to arrive early.
notes: Fixed colloquial contraction (from 'que é de'); always used with a noun, never conjugated.
ear: stress falls on the last syllable: ca-DÊ, not CÁ-de

sent1: Já vou embora, tá ficando tarde.
sent1_en: I'm going now, it's getting late.
sent2: Ela foi embora sem se despedir de ninguém.
sent2_en: She left without saying goodbye to anyone.
notes: 'ir embora' = to leave. Separately, 'embora' alone can also mean 'although' as a conjunction - a different, unrelated use.
cuidado: ⚠️ 'embora' no siempre significa 'aunque'; en la expresión 'ir embora' significa simplemente 'irse', sin relación con el sentido concesivo.
ear: nasal 'em-': sounds like [ẽj-BO-ra], not an open 'en-' like in Spanish

sent1: Ele tem um jeito engraçado de falar.
sent1_en: He has a funny way of talking.
sent2: Não tem jeito, vamos ter que esperar.
sent2_en: There's no way around it, we'll have to wait.
notes: Very common noun: way, manner, knack, or a fix/solution to something.
ear: 'j' sounds like English/French 'zh', not the Spanish jota: jeito -> [ˈʒejtu]

sent1: Ele sempre dá um jeitinho pra resolver tudo.
sent1_en: He always finds a little workaround to solve everything.
sent2: O jeitinho brasileiro nem sempre é legal.
sent2_en: The Brazilian 'jeitinho' isn't always a good thing.
notes: Diminutive of 'jeito'; refers to the cultural habit of finding informal workarounds ('jeitinho brasileiro').

sent1: Pode esquentar a comida no micro-ondas.
sent1_en: You can heat up the food in the microwave.
sent2: Relaxa, não esquenta com isso.
sent2_en: Relax, don't worry about it.
notes: 'não esquenta' is a fixed colloquial phrase for 'don't worry about it'.

gloss: to get in the way, hinder, mess up
sent1: Sai da frente, você tá atrapalhando.
sent1_en: Get out of the way, you're getting in the way.
sent2: A chuva atrapalhou todo o passeio.
sent2_en: The rain messed up the whole outing.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es 'atropellar' (arrollar con un vehículo); significa 'estorbar' o 'entorpecer'.

gloss: to mess up, make a mess of
sent1: As crianças bagunçaram o quarto inteiro.
sent1_en: The kids messed up the whole room.
sent2: Não bagunça meus papéis, por favor.
sent2_en: Don't mess up my papers, please.

sent1: Ela adora fofoca do trabalho.
sent1_en: She loves gossip from work.
sent2: Não vim aqui pra fofoca, quero falar sério.
sent2_en: I didn't come here for gossip, I want to talk seriously.

sent1: Tô com saudade da minha família.
sent1_en: I'm missing my family.
sent2: Que saudade daquele tempo de escola!
sent2_en: I really miss those school days!
notes: Famously said to have no exact translation; blends missing, nostalgia, and affection into one word.

sent1: Meu avô é muito teimoso, não muda de ideia.
sent1_en: My grandfather is very stubborn, he doesn't change his mind.
sent2: Para de ser teimosa e escuta o médico.
sent2_en: Stop being stubborn and listen to the doctor.

sent1: Ninguém gosta de puxa-saco no trabalho.
sent1_en: Nobody likes a brown-noser at work.
sent2: Ele só elogia o chefe, é um puxa-saco.
sent2_en: He only praises the boss, he's such a kiss-up.
notes: Literally 'bag-puller'; a vivid, very informal compound noun used constantly in spoken register.
ear: 'x' sounds like 'sh': puxa -> [ˈpuʃa], not like an 's' or 'ks'

gloss: to annoy, be a pain, nag
sent1: Para de encher o saco, já disse que não!
sent1_en: Stop being annoying, I already said no!
sent2: Esse barulho tá enchendo o saco de todo mundo.
sent2_en: This noise is annoying everyone.
notes: Literally 'to fill the bag'; mildly vulgar idiom, safe in casual contexts, means to annoy or nag.

gloss: annoying person, pain in the neck
sent1: Ele é tão mala que ninguém quer sair com ele.
sent1_en: He's such a pain that nobody wants to hang out with him.
sent2: Que mala essa mulher, não para de reclamar.
sent2_en: What a pain that woman is, she doesn't stop complaining.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es 'maleta'; aquí 'mala' es un adjetivo coloquial para persona pesada/molesta (parecido en espíritu a 'malo', pero no es lo mismo).

gloss: to joke around, tease, mess around
sent1: Para de zoar comigo, isso não tem graça.
sent1_en: Stop teasing me, that's not funny.
sent2: A galera ficou zoando até tarde no churrasco.
sent2_en: The crew kept joking around late at the barbecue.
notes: Can mean both 'to tease/joke' and 'to mess something up', depending on context.

sent1: A gente curtiu muito a viagem pra praia.
sent1_en: We really enjoyed the trip to the beach.
sent2: Curti sua foto nova, ficou linda.
sent2_en: I liked your new photo, it turned out beautiful.
cuidado: ⚠️ No tiene relación con 'curtir cuero/piel' del español; en portugués significa 'disfrutar' o 'dar like'.

gloss: to agree, be up for it
sent1: Você topa ir no show comigo sábado?
sent1_en: Are you up for going to the show with me on Saturday?
sent2: Ele topou ajudar a gente na mudança.
sent2_en: He agreed to help us with the move.
cuidado: ⚠️ No significa 'toparse con' (encontrarse por casualidad); aquí es 'aceptar, estar dispuesto a algo'.

sent1: Eu não saquei nada do que ele explicou.
sent1_en: I didn't get anything he explained.
sent2: Você sacou a piada ou não?
sent2_en: Did you get the joke or not?
cuidado: ⚠️ No significa 'sacar/retirar' algo; en este uso coloquial significa 'entender, pillar' una idea.

gloss: to be screwed, in deep trouble
sent1: Se ele descobrir, tamo ferrado.
sent1_en: If he finds out, we're screwed.
sent2: Esqueci a senha e agora tô ferrado.
sent2_en: I forgot the password and now I'm screwed.
notes: 'Estar ferrado' figuratively means to be in trouble/screwed; not commonly recognizable from Spanish.
ear: strong guttural r: ferrado -> [feˈʁadu], the 'r' sounds close to Spanish jota

sent1: A gente ficou batendo papo até tarde da noite.
sent1_en: We stayed chatting until late at night.
sent2: Vamos bater um papo depois do trabalho?
sent2_en: Want to have a chat after work?
notes: 'Papo' alone also means 'chat/talk', as in 'qual é o papo?' (what's up?).
